platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: docker/.env.example ships INIT_PASSWORD= empty (line 27) and SECRET_KEY= empty (line 26). The documented quickstart (docs.dify.ai/en/self-host/quick-start/docker-compose: copy .env.example to .env, docker compose up -d, then visit http://localhost/install) forces a setup wizard that creates the admin account before the app is usable. api/controllers/console/setup.py gates POST /console/api/setup with `if get_setup_status(): raise AlreadySetupError()` AND `if tenant_count > 0: raise AlreadySetupError()` AND `if not get_init_validate_status(): raise NotInitValidateError()`. After admin creation the UI and /console/api require a logged-in session (cookie/JWT). So a completed deployment is auth-on-default. CAVEAT (the load-bearing nuance): the INIT_PASSWORD gate is the ONLY barrier on first-run admin creation, and api/controllers/console/init_validate.py returns True (status "finished") when INIT_PASSWORD is unset — `if os.environ.get("INIT_PASSWORD"):` is skipped entirely and the function falls through to `return True`. Since the default .env.example leaves INIT_PASSWORD empty, a fresh instance exposed on port 80 BEFORE the operator completes /install is a claimable-admin race: the first anonymous visitor can POST /console/api/setup and become admin with no init-password challenge. Posture is therefore auth-on-default for an established install, but with a configurable-default-off first-run window driven by the empty INIT_PASSWORD default.
platforms.false_positive_check: Do NOT fingerprint on generic strings ("AI", "chat", "workflow") — they collide with thousands of unrelated SaaS hosts and trip the ~50% rule. The vendor-unique discriminator is the unauthenticated GET /console/api/setup returning a JSON body whose top-level key is exactly "step" with value "not_started" or "finished" (plus optional "setup_at" ISO timestamp). This route+schema pair is Dify-specific and not present on LangFlow/Flowise/n8n/RAGFlow. Confirm a second marker (GET /console/api/version returns {"version": ..., "release_date": ...} or /console/api/system-features returns a JSON object with sso_enforced_for_signin / enable_email_password_login keys) before labeling a host Dify. A bare 200 on /install with a Next.js shell alone is insufficient — many SPA builders serve _next. The setup-step JSON is the load-bearing confirmation. A {"step":"not_started"} read on an internet-exposed host is itself the finding: an un-bootstrapped instance whose admin is claimable.

platforms.auth_evidence: Documented quickstart sets ZERO auth env vars: README.md says `docker run -p 7860:7860 langflowai/langflow:latest` and `uv run langflow run`, both with no LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER/PASSWORD/AUTO_LOGIN. The default code value is LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=True (confirmed docs.langflow.org/api-keys-and-authentication: 'If LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=True (default) ... the visual editor automatically signs in all users as superusers'). Result: no login screen, every browser visitor lands in the flow builder as superuser. The shipped `.env.example` leaves LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=, LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER=, and LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD= all EMPTY (no admin password is provisioned by default). VERSION SPLIT for the API layer: in Langflow <1.5 the REST API was also fully unauthenticated at default (the population CVE-2025-3248 reaped); v1.5 introduced API-key enforcement; v1.6 default is AUTO_LOGIN=True + SKIP_AUTH_AUTO_LOGIN=False, so UI stays open-superuser while most /api/v1 endpoints require an x-api-key. Vendor's own warning: 'Never expose Langflow ports directly to the internet without proper security measures. Set LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=False.' Primary sources: README.md quickstart, .env.example, docs.langflow.org/api-keys-and-authentication.

default_creds.context: docker_example/docker-compose.yml ships the bundled Postgres with POSTGRES_USER=langflow / POSTGRES_PASSWORD=langflow and LANGFLOW_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://langflow:langflow@postgres:5432/langflow, with port 5432 published to the host. Not Langflow app creds (default AUTO_LOGIN needs none) but a default-credential DB exposed alongside it.
.env.example example value LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER=admin is only consulted when AUTO_LOGIN=False; at the shipped default no superuser password exists because AUTO_LOGIN=True auto-elevates every visitor.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE, current version (v3.0.1+). packages/server/src/index.ts App.config() unconditionally calls initializeJwtCookieMiddleware() and installs a global gate over every /api/v1 path: any non-whitelisted route with no valid JWT cookie AND no valid API key returns 401 {"error":"Unauthorized Access"} — and this runs for Platform.OPEN_SOURCE, not just enterprise (the license check is the only branch gated on platform type; the auth check is not). packages/server/src/enterprise/middleware/passport/index.ts handler for the whitelisted /api/v1/auth/resolve returns redirectUrl '/organization-setup' when orgService.countOrganizations()===0, i.e. a fresh install forces admin/organization creation before the app is usable, then /signin thereafter. The default docker/.env.example ships NO admin credentials (FLOWISE_USERNAME/FLOWISE_PASSWORD are gone from the current example; only commented JWT_*/EXPRESS_SESSION_SECRET/TOKEN_HASH_SECRET hints remain) — yet the first-run org-setup screen is mandatory, so a default `docker compose up -d` followed by exposing :3000 yields a setup/login wall, not an open UI. HISTORICAL CONTRAST (auth_default shifted rightward, Insight #40): NVD CVE-2025-8943 states verbatim "Flowise versions before 3.0.1 the default installation operates without authentication unless explicitly configured" — pre-3.0.1 was configurable-default-off (open unless FLOWISE_USERNAME/FLOWISE_PASSWORD env set), which is the tier that produced the 12,000+ unauth Shodan fleet. Caveat: even on the hardened current build, the WHITELIST_URLS in packages/server/src/utils/constants.ts leave /api/v1/prediction/, /api/v1/public-chatflows, /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig, /api/v1/version, /api/v1/ping and /api/v1/settings answering unauthenticated by design (chatbot-embed + SPA-boot surface), a ui-gated-api-open seam on those specific paths only.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: docker/.env (ref d32e05d, the file the quickstart tells users to run via `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d`). It sets `REGISTER_ENABLED=1` and `DISABLE_PASSWORD_LOGIN=false`. The SPA served at port 80 (nginx root `/ragflow/web/dist`) renders a login/Sign-up gate; the first registered user becomes the tenant owner. The `/api/v1` REST API (proxied to 9380 per docker/nginx/ragflow.conf.python) requires `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`, and that key can only be minted from the authenticated UI (avatar > API). So a fresh quickstart yields NO usable UI or v1 API without registering/logging in first = auth-on-default. CAVEAT that downgrades real-world posture: docker-compose.yml default `command:` includes `--enable-adminserver`, so the Admin server is ON by default, proxied at `/api/v1/admin` (nginx) and exposed on host port 9381 (ADMIN_SVR_HTTP_PORT). Per vendor admin_ui docs it ships HARDCODED default credentials admin@ragflow.io / admin, auto-created at first startup, with no forced rotation. That secondary surface is configurable-default-on-with-default-creds.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: docker/.env.example (master) ships AUTH_TOKEN commented out: `# AUTH_TOKEN="hunter2" # This is the password to your application if remote hosting.` — alongside commented `# JWT_SECRET` ("Only needed if AUTH_TOKEN is set"), `# SIG_KEY`, `# SIG_SALT`. The only active line is `SERVER_PORT=3001`. The canonical quickstart in docker/HOW_TO_USE_DOCKER.md (`docker run -d --rm -p 3001:3001 --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -v ... mintplexlabs/anythingllm`) sets NO AUTH_TOKEN and NO password. Server behavior (server/endpoints/system.js): single-user login is enforced only when AUTH_TOKEN is present in env ("disabled if in development or AUTH_TOKEN is unset"). Multi-user mode is separate, explicitly opt-in, and irreversible. Therefore a user running the documented quickstart and exposing port 3001 gets a fully unauthenticated UI AND API. Auth exists but is OFF unless the AUTH_TOKEN env var (or multi-user mode) is set => configurable-default-off.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: default .env.example at tag v0.0.317 (commit 3523665) sets `AUTHENTICATE=true`, so the legacy full-stack app (frontend:3000 + FastAPI backend-api:5050, both `ports:`-exposed in the default docker-compose.yml) does gate the API behind a Supabase HS256 JWT by default. BUT the same .env.example ships the publicly-known Supabase local-dev secrets verbatim: `JWT_SECRET_KEY=super-secret-jwt-token-with-at-least-32-characters-long`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY=eyJ...role:service_role iss:supabase-demo`, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...role:anon iss:supabase-demo` (the exact documented Supabase demo keys). backend/api/quivr_api/middlewares/auth/jwt_token_handler.py decodes with `jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=['HS256'], options={'verify_aud': False})` reading SECRET_KEY from JWT_SECRET_KEY. So an operator who runs the quickstart unchanged exposes a backend whose JWT secret is a public constant: any attacker can forge an HS256 token for any user_id and pass auth (aud unverified). Separately, auth_bearer.py honors `AUTHENTICATE=false` by returning a hardcoded admin (`UserIdentity(email='admin@quivr.app', id='39418e3b-0258-4452-af60-7acfcc1263ff')`) for every request with no credentials. The Celery Flower monitor (port 5555) ships with no auth in the default compose. Posture is configurable-default-on in name, but the shipped default secret renders it effectively forgeable-unauth. NOTE: current repo `main` is now the `quivr-core` PyPI library (`pip install quivr-core`) which is a code library with NO server/listener/auth surface; the internet-exposed population is the pre-refactor full-stack app.

platforms.auth_evidence: Primary source: settings.yaml in zylon-ai/private-gpt ships `server.auth.enabled: false` (server block: `port: ${PORT:8080}`, `auth:\n  enabled: false\n  secret: "Basic c2VjcmV0OmtleQ=="`). The companion source private_gpt/server/utils/auth.py proves the consequence: `if not settings().server.auth.enabled:` it defines `def authenticated(): return True` — a dummy dependency that always authenticates every FastAPI route. The ingested-doc, /v1/chunks retrieval, and chat endpoints are therefore fully unauth in a stock deploy. The `secret:key` base64 token in settings.yaml is only the EXAMPLE token used IF an operator flips enabled to true; it is inert by default. The classic quickstart (`PGPT_PROFILES=ollama make run`) and docker-compose bind 0.0.0.0:8001 and serve the Gradio UI + API with no login. Auth code exists and is OFF unless `server.auth.enabled: true` is set -> configurable-default-off, not open-by-default.

default_creds.context: settings.yaml server.auth.secret base64-encodes secret:key as the EXAMPLE bearer token; inert because server.auth.enabled is false by default. Only live if an operator enables auth without changing the secret.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: goldenverba/server/api.py ships NO authentication of any kind - no Depends() auth dependency, no APIKeyHeader/OAuth2/HTTPBearer, no password, no login route, no session/token. The only access control is an HTTP middleware check_same_origin (verbatim): it exempts "/api/health" outright, and for other "/api/" paths returns 403 unless the request Origin header equals str(request.base_url).rstrip("/"). base_url is derived from the client-controlled Host header, so a non-browser client that sets Origin to match the target's own host (e.g. Origin: http://TARGET:8000) bypasses it entirely - this is anti-CSRF, not authentication. The middleware is HTTP-only: BOTH WebSocket endpoints (/ws/generate_stream runs RAG queries returning ingested doc content + LLM answers; /ws/import_files ingests files) bypass it completely with zero origin/token/auth checks; /ws/import_files is gated only by `if production == "Demo": return`, and production/DEFAULT_DEPLOYMENT are UNSET in the quickstart. The default docker-compose.yml sets no Verba auth env vars at all and runs the backing Weaviate with AUTHENTICATION_ANONYMOUS_ACCESS_ENABLED: 'true'. goldenverba/.env.example contains ONLY commented-out upstream provider keys (OPENAI/COHERE/ANTHROPIC/WEAVIATE_API_KEY) - no admin password, no Verba login secret exists in the shipped config. README states Verba is "single user usage only" with "no plans on supporting multiple users or role based access." Repo is ARCHIVED/discontinued: no future security patches.

platforms.auth_evidence: Default quickstart is `docker-compose --env-file compose.env up`, which exposes cognita-frontend on :5001 and cognita-backend (FastAPI/uvicorn) on :8000 with ZERO application-level authentication. PRIMARY SOURCES: (1) backend/settings.py defines no ENABLE_AUTH / JWT_SECRET / ADMIN_PASSWORD / API_KEY field — only external-service keys (TFY_API_KEY, BRAVE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, UNSTRUCTURED_IO_API_KEY) and a CORS toggle (ALLOW_CORS default False, but the deployed compose enables a wildcard CORS config). (2) backend/server/app.py includes all routers (collection/components/data_source/rag_apps/internal + dynamic query controllers) with NO Depends(auth)/Security/middleware token check anywhere — only a health endpoint and exception handlers. (3) backend/server/routers/internal.py routes (/v1/internal/*) carry no auth dependency; the only gate on upload-to-local-directory is `if settings.LOCAL` — and compose.env ships `LOCAL=true`. (4) compose.env ships only test/infra creds (POSTGRES_USER=postgres / POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test, INFINITY_API_KEY='test', UNSTRUCTURED_IO_API_KEY='test') — none gate the web UI or REST API. A user who runs the documented quickstart and exposes :8000 or :5001 to the internet has a fully unauthenticated UI + API.

platforms.auth_evidence: Default ships auth OFF. Primary source `py/r2r/r2r.toml` [auth] section sets verbatim: provider = "r2r", require_authentication = false, require_email_verification = false, default_admin_email = "admin@example.com", default_admin_password = "change_me_immediately". The quickstart compose loads `docker/env/r2r-full.env`, which does NOT set R2R_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATION (auth absent from the entire env file), so the toml default governs unchanged. The runtime auth_wrapper in py/core/base/providers/auth.py auto-elevates anonymous callers to the default admin: `if (((not self.config.require_authentication) or public) and auth is None and api_key is None): return await self._get_default_admin_user()` where `_get_default_admin_user` resolves the user by `self.admin_email` (admin@example.com). Net result: a documented quickstart `docker compose -f compose.full.yaml --profile postgres up -d` exposes port 7272 where every /v3 REST call is served as the superuser with no credential. This is auth-exists-but-OFF-by-default, not open-by-default (the provider machinery is present and a single env/toml flip arms it).
auth_config_env: R2R_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATION (not set in docker/env/r2r-full.env; toml default require_authentication=false governs)
R2R_CONFIG_NAME=full (selects config that inherits the require_authentication=false default)
R2R_SECRET_KEY (JWT signing key; blank in template)

GET /v3/system/settings returns 200 with a config TOML dump (config_toml) and r2r_project_name when auth is default-off (superuser-only check satisfied by anonymous auto-elevation)
platforms.false_positive_check: "v3/health" and "results"/"message":"ok" are individually generic and collide with countless APIs. Disambiguate by requiring the R2R-specific COMBINATION on port 7272: (1) GET /v3/health -> 200 with exactly {"results":{"message":"ok"}} (the "results" envelope is R2R's response wrapper, not a bare {"status":"ok"}), AND (2) GET /v3/system/settings -> 200 returning a JSON object containing "config" + "prompts" + "r2r_project_name" keys (config dumped from to_toml). A host that returns the health 200 but 401/403 on /v3/system/settings is either a hardened R2R (auth armed) or a lookalike; a host returning config_toml + r2r_project_name is positively R2R with default-off auth. Do not fingerprint on bare "RAG"/"retrieval"/"chat" strings. Port 7273 (Next.js dashboard) alone is a weak tell since it is a generic Next.js app on :3000 internally; anchor on the 7272 /v3 envelope instead.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the canonical docker-compose.yml in khoj-ai/khoj (master, the file the quickstart at docs.khoj.dev/get-started/setup tells users to download and `docker-compose up`) ends the `server` service with: `command: --host="0.0.0.0" --port=42110 -vv --anonymous-mode --non-interactive`. The `--anonymous-mode` flag is hardcoded into the shipped command. Confirmed in source (src/khoj/configure.py auth backend): with anonymous_mode True the backend short-circuits all auth — `if state.anonymous_mode: user = await self.khojuser_manager.filter(username="default")... return AuthCredentials(["authenticated","premium"]), AuthenticatedKhojUser(user)`. Every UI and REST request is silently authenticated as the `default` premium user; no login, no API key. The compose also binds 0.0.0.0:42110 directly. The compose comment block itself warns: "Uncomment the necessary lines below to make your instance publicly accessible. Proceed with caution, especially if you are using anonymous mode." A user who runs the documented quickstart and exposes 42110 to the internet gets a fully unauth UI+API. (Note: only when anonymous_mode is False does configure.py mount auth_router and return UnauthenticatedUser() for unauthed requests — but the shipped default has it on.)

platforms.auth_evidence: Default `.env.example` (the file the docs' quickstart copies verbatim: `cp .env.example .env`) ships `ALLOW_EMAIL_LOGIN=true` and `ALLOW_REGISTRATION=true` with `HOST=localhost PORT=3080`. The official docker quickstart docs state: "you'll see the login page where you can register your account - the first user created becomes the admin." So authentication IS forced at the UI/chat layer (no anonymous chat; login required), which makes the base posture configurable-default-on. The load-bearing caveat: registration is OPEN by default and there is NO admin-approval gate and NO privileged first-run setup token. On a fresh internet-exposed instance the FIRST self-registered user becomes admin; if the operator has not yet registered, an attacker who reaches the port first claims admin. `.env.example` also ships SHARED, PUBLISHED-IN-REPO default JWT_SECRET / JWT_REFRESH_SECRET / CREDS_KEY / CREDS_IV values that many operators never rotate (forgeable sessions / decryptable stored API keys). docker-compose.yml runs mongodb with `mongod --noauth`, so the backing datastore is unauthenticated by container default (only network-isolated, not access-controlled).

default_creds.context: No default admin account ships. The first self-registered user becomes admin (docs: 'the first user created becomes the admin'). Risk is the OPEN registration gate plus first-user-wins admin assignment, not a hardcoded credential.

.env.example ships this exact JWT signing secret (and JWT_REFRESH_SECRET eaa5191f..., CREDS_KEY f34be427..., CREDS_IV e2341419...). Operators who copy .env.example and never rotate run forgeable session tokens and repo-decryptable stored upstream API keys.

Supports comma-separated values" with default value shown as "-" (unset/empty). When ACCESS_CODE is empty/unset the instance is accessible without authentication. The classic client-side quickstart docker run command does NOT include it: `docker run -d -p 3210:3210 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-xxxx --name lobe-chat lobehub/lobe-chat` yields a fully unauth UI+API; ACCESS_CODE is only added as an OPTIONAL `-e ACCESS_CODE=lobe66` flag in the "add a password" example. platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the quickstart artifact (docs "Docker Compose" page tells users to `curl -O .../infra-as-code/docker-compose.yml` then `docker compose up`). That default infra-as-code/docker-compose.yml file (1) carries the header comment "Do not expose this configuration to the internet... This is NOT a production configuration", (2) publishes the `app` service as `3000:7703`, (3) sets `DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE: eyJhbGci...` on the app service (a hardcoded unsigned JWT: sub=1234567890, name="John Doe", email=john@acme.org), and (4) ships NO oauth2-proxy / barricade / keycloak / nginx-auth service. The auth model lives entirely in crates/web-server/jwt.rs: the Jwt FromRequestParts extractor does `if let Ok(override_token) = std::env::var("DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE") { Some(override_token) } else { ...X-Forwarded-Access-Token header... }`. When the override is set EVERY request is authenticated as that identity, and even on the header path the token signature is NEVER verified (it base64-decodes the middle segment and reads sub/email out of the JSON). Real authentication is delegated to an upstream oauth2-proxy/barricade that injects X-Forwarded-User / X-Forwarded-Email (see config.rs `enable_barricade = env::var("ENABLE_BARRICADE").is_ok()` and README architecture diagram User -> Nginx -> OAuth2 Proxy -> Rust Server). Net: a user who runs the documented quickstart and exposes port 3000 gets a fully unauthenticated UI+API where every visitor is the baked-in admin "John Doe" - auth EXISTS (oauth2-proxy) but is OFF in the shipped default and must be added for production.

Discriminator: the Bionic app is a Rust/axum server that on a default quickstart answers /v1/chat/completions and /v1/models OpenAI-style on port 3000 (->7703) WITHOUT any Authorization header because DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE is set and no oauth2-proxy fronts it. A production Bionic behind oauth2-proxy/barricade returns 302->IdP on /console and is NOT the open-default case - so an unauth 200 on /console with the Bionic asset bundle is the load-bearing confirmation.

Design exposure (not a CVE): crates/web-server/jwt.rs never verifies the JWT signature - it base64-decodes the payload and trusts sub/email; signature validation is wholly delegated to the upstream oauth2-proxy. If the proxy is removed/misconfigured, any attacker-crafted unsigned JWT or any X-Forwarded-User/X-Forwarded-Email header is accepted as identity.
Design exposure (not a CVE): DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE env var ships SET in the default quickstart compose, authenticating all traffic as a fixed admin identity with no login or rate limit.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: backend/open_webui/config.py sets `WEBUI_AUTH = os.getenv('WEBUI_AUTH', 'True').lower() == 'true'` — auth is ON when the env var is unset. The canonical quickstart `docker run -d -p 3000:8080 -v open-webui:/app/backend/data --name open-webui --restart always ghcr.io/open-webui/open-webui:main` (README.md) passes NO WEBUI_AUTH override, so the True default holds. The default docker-compose.yaml contains no WEBUI_AUTH and only an empty `WEBUI_SECRET_KEY=`. The hardening doc (docs.openwebui.com/getting-started/advanced-topics/hardening/) confirms runtime behavior verbatim: "Signup is open only until the first user registers, who becomes the administrator" and "Open WebUI is built for private, trusted networks ... Do not expose it directly to the public internet without an additional access control layer in front of it." NUANCE that downgrades the protection: with WEBUI_AUTH=True the first run shows a setup/login form, but on a FRESH internet-exposed instance ENABLE_SIGNUP defaults True (config.py: `os.getenv('ENABLE_SIGNUP','True')`) and the first registrant becomes admin — a first-user-becomes-admin race, not a pre-set credential. DEFAULT_USER_ROLE defaults 'pending' so subsequent signups are quarantined. Classified auth-on-default (the documented quickstart forces a setup/login before use), NOT open-by-default; the open state requires the operator to explicitly set WEBUI_AUTH=False.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: document/public/deploy/docker/main/cn/docker-compose.pg.yml (the pgvector compose the quickstart install.sh pulls). The fastgpt-app service sets `DEFAULT_ROOT_PSW: *x-default-root-psw` where the anchor is `x-default-root-psw: &x-default-root-psw '1234'`, username `root`. The inline comment states root password "重启后会强制重置该密码成环境变量值" (force-reset to the env value on every container restart), so the login is gated but the credential is a universal, predictable default (root/1234). The file header literally warns "建议修改账密后再运行" (recommended to change the credentials before running), confirming it ships insecure-by-default. Posture is auth-on-default (there IS a login wall) but the effective security is near-open because the seeded credential is identical across every unmodified deploy. Additional hardcoded shared secrets in the same default file: `TOKEN_KEY: fastgpt`, `FILE_TOKEN_KEY: filetokenkey`, `AES256_SECRET_KEY: fastgptsecret`, `ROOT_KEY: 'fastgpt-xxx'`, plus DB creds myusername/mypassword (mongo+redis) and minioadmin/minioadmin (object store). Caveat lowering this below the pure auth-on-default ideal: the unauth endpoint /api/common/system/getInitData returns feConfigs+modelProviders to callers with no token (catch-block fallback in projects/app/src/pages/api/common/system/getInitData.ts), so a slice of the API answers pre-login (ui-gated-api-open behavior on that route).

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: official README.md and docs.maxkb.pro/quick_start/ both ship the SAME quickstart: `docker run -d --name=maxkb --restart=always -p 8080:8080 -v ~/.maxkb:/var/lib/postgresql/data -v ~/.python-packages:/opt/maxkb/app/sandbox/python-packages 1panel/maxkb` and then instruct: log in at http://server_ip:8080 with default credentials user `admin` / pass `MaxKB@123..`, then 'Change the default password after logging in.' So the first-run posture is auth-on-default (a Django login gates the UI and the management API), NOT open-by-default. The load-bearing caveat: MaxKB ships a HARDCODED, globally-known shared default credential (admin / MaxKB@123..) baked into the image's bootstrap rather than generating a random one or forcing a setup wizard. An operator who runs the documented quickstart and exposes 8080 to the internet without changing the password is one credential-stuff away from full admin (model API keys, RAG corpus, agent flows). The thin 'login required' shell therefore degrades in practice to near-open for the not-uncommon default-password deployment. Reinforced by shipped-version auth gaps in primary advisories: GHSA-r3j3-j58q-rjpp 'Webhook Trigger Authentication Bypass' (CWE-287/CWE-306, missing auth on the webhook trigger endpoint) and GHSA-2jmj-gwvg-3gp2 'Broken Access Control in MaxKB OSS URL Fetch API' — both are routes that answered unauthenticated in released builds.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the quickstart (`git clone ... && cd QAnything && bash run.sh`) runs docker-compose-linux.yaml, which on master maps the app `"8777:8777"` and on qanything-v2 runs it under `network_mode: "host"` on 8777, with NO auth-related env var on the qanything_local service (only NCCL_LAUNCH_MODE, GPUID*, USER_IP/Gateway_IP). The official docs/API.md states the access URLs are `http://your_host:8777/qanything/` (frontend) and `http://your_host:8777/api/` with NO Authorization header, token, or API key on any endpoint; user identity is a free-text body parameter only: "if you don't need to distinguish different users, you can pass user_id='zzp'". This means a quickstart deployment exposed to the internet yields a fully unauthenticated UI AND REST API. Supporting backing-service tells in the same compose: elasticsearch `xpack.security.enabled=false`, mysql `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=123456`, minio `MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=minioadmin / MINIO_SECRET_KEY=minioadmin`. In v2 (README): LLM model + OpenAI-compatible API key/base_url are "automatically saved in the front end settings" (configured via the unauth web UI, retrievable via get_bot_info), not via a protected env file.

platforms.false_positive_check: Do not match on generic strings like "QA", "chat", or "Anything" — they collide with thousands of unrelated apps. Anchor on the co-occurrence of (a) the literal path segment `/api/local_doc_qa/` AND (b) port 8777 serving a frontend at `/qanything/` with `<title>QAnything</title>`. The decisive non-FP confirmation is a POST to `/api/local_doc_qa/list_knowledge_base` with body `{"user_id":"zzp"}` returning a JSON envelope `{"code":200,"msg":"success","data":[...]}` — the `local_doc_qa` route prefix and the JSON code/msg/data envelope are vendor-unique. A bare 200 on `/` or a reachable 8777 banner alone is insufficient (could be any host-networked container); require the local_doc_qa JSON envelope. platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: flowsettings.py L95-97 sets `KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT = config("KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT", default=True, cast=bool)` -> user management ON by default. libs/ktem/ktem/main.py gates the UI: the Chat/Files/Settings tabs are rendered with `visible=not self.f_user_management` and `toggle_login_visibility` hides every tab except the login tab while `not user_id`; a LoginPage tab is added first when `f_user_management` is true. libs/ktem/ktem/pages/resources/user.py `UserManagement.__init__` auto-seeds the admin on first run: `usn = flowsettings.KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN; pwd = flowsettings.KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_PASSWORD; create_user(usn, pwd)` (create_user is a no-op if the user already exists). flowsettings.py defaults both to "admin": `KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN` default="admin", `KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_PASSWORD` default="admin". NET POSTURE: the documented quickstart (`docker run ... ghcr.io/cinnamon/kotaemon:main-full` or `python app.py`) forces a login wall before any chat/document access, so it is auth-on-default, NOT open-by-default. The weakness is the credential, not the absence of a gate: the publicly-documented README states "Default username and password are both `admin`", with NO forced first-run password change and NO rate limiting in login.py (sha256-no-salt, DB lookup, gr.Warning on fail). KH_DEMO_MODE (default=False) would bypass login by auto-creating random users, but is off in the default config and so does not affect default posture.

default_creds.context: First-run admin auto-seeded by UserManagement.__init__ from KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN/_PASSWORD (both default 'admin' in flowsettings.py). README states verbatim: 'Default username and password are both admin.' No forced rotation, no rate limit on the login form.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE, three corroborating files at github.com/ragapp/ragapp (main). (1) README.md states verbatim: "Just the RAGapp container doesn't come with any authentication layer by design. This is the task of an API Gateway routing the traffic to RAGapp." The README's own quickstart is `docker run -p 8000:8000 ragapp/ragapp` and exposes Admin UI /admin, Chat UI /, API /docs with no login step. (2) src/ragapp/main.py mounts the management router at prefix `/api/management` with NO auth dependency; the only middleware anywhere is `Depends(request_limit_middleware)` on the chat and upload routers (a rate limiter, not authentication). (3) src/ragapp/backend/routers/management/config.py defines GET/POST `/chat`, `/models`, `/is_configured`, `/models/list` with no auth dependency on any route. The default deployments/single/docker-compose.yml sets no auth variable for the ragapp service and explicitly `QDRANT_API_KEY=""`. Result: running the documented quickstart and exposing :8000 yields a fully unauthenticated admin UI AND REST API. The vendor explicitly off-loads auth to an external API gateway the operator must add; nothing in the container ships it.

To avoid the ~50%-rule false positive, anchor on the RAGapp-UNIQUE management surface: (1) the `/api/management/config`, `/api/management/agents`, `/api/management/reranker`, `/api/management/llamacloud` route family is RAGapp-specific (plain create-llama has no /api/management); (2) the literal admin app at `/admin` serving a Next.js RAGapp builder UI plus the string "RAGapp" in the HTML; (3) presence of /api/management AND /api/chat AND /admin together. A host that has /api/chat but no /api/management is upstream create-llama, NOT RAGapp - do not tag it. Confirm by reading the OpenAPI at /openapi.json for the /api/management/* path group.

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the default `morphik.toml` shipped in morphik-org/morphik-core ships, verbatim, `[auth] jwt_algorithm = "HS256" / bypass_auth_mode = true  # Enabled by default for easier local development / dev_user_id = "dev_user"`, and `[morphik] mode = "self_hosted"`, `[service] environment = "development"`. `core/auth_utils.py` verify_token: when `settings.bypass_auth_mode` is true it returns `AuthContext(user_id=settings.dev_user_id, app_id=None)` with NO Authorization header checked, so every request (UI and REST/API) is served as the dev_user with no token. `core/config.py` loads `bypass_auth_mode` straight from the toml and only raises "JWT_SECRET_KEY is required" when bypass is OFF; `dev_mode` is a backward-compat alias property for `bypass_auth_mode`. The canonical installer `install_docker.sh` (run via `curl -sSL https://install.morphik.ai | bash`) downloads `docker-compose.run.yml` (publishes `8000:8000`) plus this `morphik.toml`, and prompts "enter a secure LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD (or press Enter to skip for local-only access)" — pressing Enter (the default, lowest-friction path) prints "No LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD provided - enabling authentication bypass (bypass_auth_mode=true)". So a user who runs the documented quickstart and accepts defaults gets a fully unauthenticated UI+API; auth exists but is OFF unless the operator sets a LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD / flips bypass_auth_mode=false. Classified configurable-default-off (it skews open-by-default in practice because the install prompt defaults to skip).

platforms.auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: authz/authz.go embeds a Casbin policy with three roles (g, admin, user; g, user, anonymous). The unauthenticated 'anonymous' role is explicitly granted on a whitelist of REST routes — literal policy lines include `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-chats`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-messages`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-message`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-chat`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-global-stores`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-store-names`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-store`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-providers`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-vector`, `p, anonymous, *, /api/get-tasks`, plus WRITE paths `p, anonymous, *, /api/add-chat`, `/api/add-message`, `/api/add-task`. routers/authz_filter.go applies default-DENY for everything else BUT short-circuits the OpenAI-compatible proxy entirely: `if controllerName == 'chat/completions' { return }` — so /api/chat/completions answers with NO authz check at all. The React admin SPA gates the browser UI behind a Casdoor login, but these JSON API routes answer without a session cookie/token, which is the textbook ui-gated-api-open split. NOTE on restraint: object/provider.go masks ClientSecret/ProviderKey/UserKey to '***' for non-admin callers via GetMaskedProvider, so the anonymous get-providers path does NOT leak raw upstream LLM keys — do not overclaim key exposure. Default install creds are admin (built-in/admin / password '123' on demos, 'admin123' on the all-in-one image), and docker-compose.yml ships MySQL root password '123456' with port 3306 published to the host.
auth_config_env: isDemoMode (conf/app.conf, default false; when true authz_filter restricts to a POST whitelist)
clientId / clientSecret / issuer (conf/app.conf, empty in repo source; populated from Casdoor in the all-in-one image)

The empty-string bind `("", 8000)` listens on ALL interfaces. There is NO @login_required, no token/API-key check, no session-auth, no middleware anywhere in the file — every GET/POST/DELETE route (incl. /sessions, /indexes/{id}/upload, /chat) answers unauthenticated. CORS is wide open: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`. backend/database.py creates only sessions/messages/session_documents/indexes/index_documents/session_indexes tables — NO users/auth/password table exists in the schema. docker.env ships ONLY OLLAMA_HOST/NODE_ENV/NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/RAG_API_URL — zero credential or auth-toggle vars. docker-compose.yml publishes 3000/8000/8001 (and 11434 under the with-ollama profile with OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0) with no auth proxy. README quickstart is `./start-docker.sh` then `open http://localhost:3000` — no setup/login/first-run admin step. This is independently confirmed by CVE-2026-5000 (CWE-306 Missing Authentication, CVSS 3.1 7.3) filed against backend/server.py at this exact commit.

The reliable discriminator is the BACKEND API on :8000: GET /health returning a JSON body containing the exact key triad \"ollama_running\" + \"available_models\" + \"database_stats\" together (the co-occurrence of ollama_running with database_stats is unique to this codebase). platforms.auth_evidence: Architecture is Next.js (port 3000) fronting Supabase Auth + Postgres-with-RLS; auth is INHERITED from Supabase, not built in-app. The web UI is login-gated: app/[locale]/page.tsx renders only a "Start Chatting" button linking to /login, and app/[locale]/layout.tsx wraps the app shell as `{session ? <GlobalState>{children}</GlobalState> : children}` so no usable UI renders without a session. The REST/chat/retrieval API routes are NOT unauthenticated: every model route calls getServerProfile() in lib/server/server-chat-helpers.ts, which runs `supabase.auth.getUser()` and `throw new Error("User not found")` when there is no valid Supabase session cookie. Data isolation is Supabase RLS (e.g. setup migration policy `TO authenticated USING (auth.uid()::text = owner_id::text)`). HOWEVER, the documented-quickstart Supabase config ships the access-restriction control OFF: supabase/config.toml sets `[auth] enable_signup = true`, `[auth.email] enable_signup = true`, and `[auth.email] enable_confirmations = false`; and .env.local.example leaves `EMAIL_DOMAIN_WHITELIST=` and `EMAIL_WHITELIST=` EMPTY. Net effect on a default exposed instance: any internet user can self-register an account with no email confirmation and immediately use the app — including spending the operator's server-side LLM API keys set via OPENAI_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env vars (which getServerProfile's addApiKeysToProfile injects globally for all users). The hardening lever (set EMAIL_WHITELIST / EMAIL_DOMAIN_WHITELIST, or disable signup in the Supabase dashboard per README "Configure Auth" step) exists but is off in the shipped defaults. Quoted: supabase/config.toml lines `enable_signup = true` / `enable_confirmations = false`; .env.local.example `EMAIL_DOMAIN_WHITELIST=` / `EMAIL_WHITELIST=`.

Exclude the v1.0 'legacy' branch lineage (localStorage architecture, no /login, no Supabase) and unrelated WordPress/Oracle 'chatbot' products surfaced by keyword search."

default_creds.context: Hardcoded Supabase DEMO service_role JWT (iss:supabase-demo) committed in supabase/migrations/20240108234540_setup.sql at the delete_storage_object() function. This is the well-known default local-Supabase service-role key. If an operator exposes the Supabase Kong/REST gateway (default container supabase_kong_chatbotui:8000, host port 54321) without rotating it, this key grants full service_role (RLS-bypassing) DB+storage access. The README only instructs replacing it for HOSTED deployments; local-quickstart instances keep the demo key.

No shipped login credential, but config.toml enable_signup=true + enable_confirmations=false + empty EMAIL_WHITELIST means an exposed instance lets any visitor create their own account with no approval = effective default-credential equivalent for app access.

The quickstart instantiates VannaFlaskApp(vn) without passing auth, so every /api/v0/* route (generate_sql, run_sql, get_training_data, train, remove_training_data) answers fully unauthenticated; run() binds host="0.0.0.0", port=8084, debug=self.debug with debug defaulting to True. The canonical vanna-flask quickstart repo (vanna-ai/vanna-flask/app.py) is even more explicit: NO auth decorator on any of its 14 @app.route('/api/v0/...') handlers, ending with app.run(debug=True) (Flask default port 5000). v2 (src/vanna/servers/flask/app.py VannaFlaskServer.run) sets defaults host="0.0.0.0", port=5000, debug=False, enables CORS by default (cors_config.get("enabled", True)) and registers /api/vanna/v2/chat_sse + /api/vanna/v2/chat_poll with NO auth layer in the server factory (authentication is opt-in via a custom UserResolver the developer must wire up). missing_reasoning:
CATEGORY BOUNDARY (Cat-34): the self-hostable low-code/visual layer that wires an LLM to private documents/data, i.e. (a) deployable via docker/helm/binary, (b) a RAG product OR a visual-LLM-app builder that ships a turnkey document-chat experience, (c) with meaningful real-world self-host population. The unit of inclusion is a deployable PRODUCT (server + UI/config surface), not a library you must write code against.

INCLUDED beyond the 30 already-researched: the four big Chinese/vendor RAG knowledge-base products (FastGPT, MaxKB, QAnything, Casibase), the document-chat UIs with built-in RAG (Open WebUI, Kotaemon, h2oGPT, LocalGPT, Chatbot UI), the LlamaIndex/Morphik turnkey RAG containers (RAGapp, Morphik), and Vanna as the text-to-SQL RAG subtype.

DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED:
1. Already in the tome corpus or already researched this round: onyx (the Danswer rename, present in tome/platforms/onyx.json) and privategpt (in tome AND in the researched list) — both dropped; all 30 researched slugs dropped.
2. App FRAMEWORKS/SDKs, not products: Chainlit and Reflex are conversational-AI frameworks you must write the app in (peer of langserve, already in tome) — they ship no turnkey RAG product, so they dilute the class and are excluded. Same logic keeps raw LangChain/LlamaIndex core libraries out (RAGapp/Morphik are their deployable product wrappers and ARE in).
3. Not-a-server libraries: Microsoft GraphRAG core is an indexer/library; the community 'graphrag-server' wrappers have no unified self-host population, so excluded. Dataline is a thin single-author DB-chat app with a thin population — held out as below the 'meaningful population' bar (revisit if it grows).
4. Adjacent classes that are NOT this category: raw vector DBs (chromadb/qdrant/weaviate/milvus/lancedb/marqo — all already in tome), raw inference servers (vllm/ollama/tgi/sglang/llamacpp/lmdeploy — in tome), guardrails (lakera/presidio/openguardrails — in tome), pure agent-orchestration frameworks where RAG is incidental (autogen — in tome), and coding assistants (continue-dev/tabby/sourcegraph-cody — in tome, different class). Vanna is the one boundary stretch: RAG-over-SQL not RAG-over-docs, but it is a deployable RAG product with real self-host population, so it earns inclusion as a labeled subtype rather than an exclusion.

Net: 12 net-new platforms, none overlapping the tome corpus or the researched set.

attack_surface_ranking.rationale: Open-by-default NoAuth() plus unauth /api/v0/run_sql executes arbitrary SQL against the connected production database. Asset is not the corpus, it is the live DB itself.
Bare BaseHTTPRequestHandler with no auth table by design, bound to all interfaces. CVE-2026-5000 confirms unauth on every route plus co-located unauth Ollama on 11434.

Open-by-default REST API with client-supplied free-text user_id. Unauth get_file_base64 returns raw ingested files; user_id iteration enumerates other tenants. Host-mode v2 leaks ES/Milvus/MySQL/MinIO.
AUTO_LOGIN=True default lands every visitor as superuser; superuser equals arbitrary Python equals RCE. Pre-1.3.0 wormable unauth RCE (CISA KEV, Flodrix botnet) still populates the fleet.
Open-by-default UI+API with zero-click RCE chain (CVE-2025-27519 + CVE-2025-27518, public PoC). Repo archived, no future patches. LLM keys recoverable via the RCE.
Shipped --anonymous-mode makes every visitor a premium default user. Asset is a personal second-brain knowledge base, PII-dense by definition. High exposure rate from the hardcoded flag.
Open-by-default with unauth /deploy_files accepting arbitrary Python pipeline_wrapper.py = direct RCE. Niche port 1416 lowers noise, raises confidence on hits.
Pre-3.0.1 fully-unauth UI+API is the 12,000-host fleet behind CVE-2025-8943 (CISA KEV, actively exploited). Current builds gated but population skews old.
Casbin policy grants anonymous read AND write on chats/stores plus /chat/completions authz bypass. ui-gated-api-open with a write primitive (RAG poison) wide open.
WebSocket RAG endpoints bypass the only middleware entirely, zero auth. Archived/EOL, paired Weaviate runs anonymous. Read and import both reachable.
Default require_authentication=false auto-elevates anonymous callers to superuser; /v3/system/settings dumps the full config TOML including every upstream provider key.
bypass_auth_mode=true shipped and the installer defaults to skip-password, so happy-path deploys are unauth full read/write across the corpus and graph.
Vendor explicitly ships no auth (gateway is your job). Unauth /api/management read+write on config/files/agents; co-bundled Qdrant on 6333 with empty API key.
Open-by-default Gradio UI plus unauth OpenAI proxy on 5000 (LLMjacking). Private-doc chat means ingested corpus is the whole point and is exposed.
auth-on-default but root/1234 shipped and force-reset every restart, plus unauth SSRF proxy (CVE-2026-34162) exfiltrates keys and reaches backing stores. China-self-host fleet.
server.auth.enabled=false default installs always-true authenticated(); /v1/chunks returns verbatim ingested doc text unauth. Quickstart binds 0.0.0.0:8001.
Open Supabase self-signup default plus hardcoded demo service_role JWT in migration. Self-registered users burn the operator's globally-injected LLM keys.
ACCESS_CODE unset by default leaves UI+API open; forgeable X-lobe-chat-auth and unauth /webapi/proxy SSRF. Many forks dilute fingerprint but population is large.
Quickstart bakes DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE so every visitor is admin John Doe, and the JWT signature is never verified. Lower deployed count than the chat giants.
AUTH_TOKEN commented out in the quickstart yields fully unauth UI+API. Heavy CVE history (multiple critical path-traversal-to-RCE). Large install base.
auth-on-default but globally-identical admin/MaxKB@123.. baked into the image. Gated on a credential-stuff, so exposure rate is high but one step removed from raw unauth.
auth-on-default with admin/admin shipped and no forced rotation, plus CVE-2025-53358 path traversal reads .env once logged in. Gradio fingerprint needs the ktem co-marker.
configurable-default-on but ships the public Supabase demo JWT secret, so HS256 tokens are forgeable for any user_id. Legacy full-stack is the exposed population; new repo is a library.
auth IS forced (login required) but registration is open and first-user-wins admin; repo-default JWT/CREDS keys decrypt stored provider keys. Strong gate, secret-rotation gap.
True auth-on-default with setup wall, but ENABLE_SIGNUP default plus first-registrant-admin race on fresh hosts. Largest install base in the category; most are hardened.
auth-on-default for completed installs; exposure is the narrow first-run claimable-admin window from empty INIT_PASSWORD. Real but time-boxed and detection-only.
auth-on-default with self-registration open and an admin server ON by default (admin@ragflow.io/admin). Highest barrier of the set; main app needs a real account first.

cross_platform_observations: Port 3000 is the category collision hub: Flowise, Cognita, Quivr, BionicGPT, Open WebUI, FastGPT, LocalGPT, and Chatbot UI all default to it. Port 3000 alone is worthless as a tell; it collides with Grafana, Next.js dev servers, and generic Node. Every dork in this set anchors on a vendor-unique JSON route or HTML string, never the port.

Port 7860 is the Gradio cluster: Langflow, PrivateGPT, Kotaemon, and h2oGPT all ride it. A bare Gradio fingerprint trips the ~50% rule against tens of thousands of HuggingFace Spaces. Each requires a vendor co-marker (ktem_app_data, /v1/ingest, h2oGPT title, /api/v1/version) layered on the Gradio base.

ui-gated-api-open is a recurring structural failure, not a one-off: Verba (WebSockets bypass the same-origin middleware), Casibase (Casbin anonymous whitelist + /chat/completions short-circuit), plus seam-level instances in Flowise (whitelisted /api/v1/prediction, /public-chatflows), FastGPT (unauth getInitData), and Open WebUI (unauth /api/config leaking modelSpecs). The browser shows a login, the JSON API does not enforce it. Fingerprint and verify at the API layer, never trust the UI gate.

Default-credential clusters dominate the auth-on-default tier and degrade it to near-open. Globally-identical shipped logins: FastGPT root/1234, MaxKB admin/MaxKB@123.., Kotaemon admin/admin, RAGFlow admin@ragflow.io/admin, Casibase built-in/admin/123 + admin/admin123. The gate exists; the key is published.

Co-located backing stores with default creds are the lateral-movement layer the app auth never covers. RAGFlow ships every store at literal infini_rag_flow (ES/MySQL/MinIO/Redis). QAnything ships minioadmin/minioadmin + root/123456 + ES security off. Quivr, LibreChat, Morphik, Cognita, BionicGPT, Khoj, Chatbot UI all bundle Postgres/pgvector with a default password and frequently host-map the port. The app gate is the front door; the stores are the open windows.

Repo-default cryptographic secrets shipped in .env.example are a category-wide token-forgery surface. LibreChat ships exact JWT_SECRET/CREDS_KEY values. Quivr ships the public Supabase demo HS256 secret. Chatbot UI commits the demo service_role JWT in a migration. Morphik ships your-secret-key-here. FastGPT ships TOKEN_KEY=fastgpt + AES256_SECRET_KEY=fastgptsecret. Operators copy and never rotate, so sessions are forgeable and stored provider keys are decryptable.

The read primitive and the poison-write primitive are siblings on the same unauth router across the category: Hayhooks /deploy_files, RAGapp /api/management/files, Verba /ws/import_files, Casibase /api/add-message, Vanna /api/v0/train, LocalGPT /upload. RAG-poisoning is one URL from corpus-read on most exposed hosts. This is the load-bearing restraint constraint for the category.

Auth-on-default strengthens across OSS generations under disclosure pressure (Insight #40), confirmed by version splits: Flowise pre-3.0.1 open -> 3.0.1+ forced org-setup; Langflow <1.5 unauth API -> 1.5 API-key enforcement -> 1.6 SKIP_AUTH_AUTO_LOGIN; FastGPT pre-4.14.9.5 NoSQL login bypass -> Zod-validated. The exposed population is dominated by stale builds, so version banner reads (CVE scoping) are the highest-value verify-stage probe.

Unauth OpenAI-compatible proxies are a shared LLMjacking surface: h2oGPT :5000, Hayhooks /v1/chat/completions, Casibase /api/chat/completions, BionicGPT /v1/*, Flowise /api/v1/prediction, Lobe Chat /webapi/chat. Anyone reaching the port spends the operator's upstream tokens. A bare /v1/* is NOT a category fingerprint (LiteLLM/vLLM/Ollama/LocalAI all answer it); require the management UI route on the same host.

China-origin self-host cluster shares deployment tells: FastGPT (Aliyun Hangzhou registry, root/1234), QAnything (NetEase Youdao, BCEmbedding), RAGFlow (TZ=Asia/Shanghai, Huawei/Aliyun mirrors), MaxKB (FIT2CLOUD, often co-deployed with 1Panel/JumpServer). These pivot together on operator host and registry attribution.
